question: What distinguishes the rate(...) and irate(...) query functions in Prometheus?
correct_answer: rate(...) calc avg rate of change of a time series over the specified time range, irate(...) calc avg rate of change of a time series at the last 2 data points
incorrect_answers: irate averages over the whole range; rate uses last 2 samples
rate is only for gauges; irate only for histograms

question: What distinguishes the rate(...) and deriv(...) query functions in Prometheus?
correct_answer: deriv(...) operates on gauge and rate(...) operates on counter
incorrect_answers: deriv is for counters; rate is for gauges
